Ansorum
  • Docs
  • Themes
  • GitHub
{% block content %}

Authoritative answers for humans and agents

Compile one answer corpus into HTML, Markdown, machine indexes, and governed AI-facing outputs.

Get started

Canonical answers

Ansorum is designed around answer units instead of generic pages, so teams can decide which answer is authoritative and reuse it across human and machine channels.

Agent-readable by default

Publish canonical Markdown, `llms.txt`, scoped packs, and machine indexes from the same source corpus instead of maintaining parallel AI-facing content.

Governed AI exposure

Control what agents can see, what stays hidden, and what is summary-only so AI delivery is explicit rather than accidental.

Audit and eval loops

Ansorum is meant to catch stale, duplicate, conflicting, or weak answers before publish and evaluate whether the corpus actually answers real questions well.

Git-native workflow

Keep content in Git, review changes in branches, and compile outputs statically without introducing a heavy application platform.

Built on a proven compiler base

This fork reuses strong parts of Zola's Rust implementation, but the product direction is now explicitly answer-first rather than a general-purpose static site engine.

{% endblock content %}
